label: task_profile_python_desktop_tool.json
description: Desktop-Tool profile — fokussiert auf Code-Qualität ohne Business-Application-Overhead. Keine DB, kein Deployment, keine Dokumentationspflicht.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Architecture / Project Structure
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Architecture / Project Structure

Area guidance (desktop-scoped):
Check: (1) no circular imports, (2) entry point is thin (no business logic in __main__), (3) module boundaries respect single-responsibility. Adapter interfaces and __all__ enforcement are optional for desktop tools.

Key tools for this area:
- pylint --disable=all --enable=cyclic-import
- importlab

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: apply either code restructuring or brief inline comments for this focus area.
- If adjacent issues are found, list them as dependencies but do not widen scope.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Code Style and Linting
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Code Style and Linting

Area guidance:
Check: (1) codebase passes ruff or flake8 without suppressions, (2) imports sorted consistently, (3) line length within project limit (≤ 99 chars), (4) no unused imports or dead code, (5) consistent docstring style (public functions only — internal helpers optional).

Key tools for this area:
- ruff (preferred)
- black
- flake8 + isort

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: apply formatting and linting fixes.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Type Safety and API Contracts
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Type Safety and API Contracts

Area guidance:
Check: (1) public functions have type annotations, (2) structured data uses dataclass or TypedDict instead of plain dicts, (3) Optional types narrowed before use. Full mypy --strict is optional — aim for practical coverage on public interfaces.

Key tools for this area:
- mypy
- pyright

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Error Handling
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Error Handling

Area guidance:
Check: (1) no bare except or silent swallowing, (2) re-raises use raise X from Y, (3) cleanup in finally or context managers, (4) top-level handler logs traceback and exits with non-zero code. A full custom exception hierarchy is optional — at minimum distinguish fatal vs. recoverable errors.

Key tools for this area:
- pylint W0703
- bandit B110

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Logging and Diagnostics
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Logging and Diagnostics

Area guidance (desktop-scoped):
Check: (1) logging.getLogger(__name__) used instead of print() in production paths, (2) log levels used appropriately, (3) no secrets or PII in log messages. Structured/JSON logging and log rotation are optional for desktop tools.

Key tools for this area:
- logging (stdlib)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: File I/O and Atomic Writes
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
File I/O and Atomic Writes

Area guidance:
Check: (1) writes use write-to-temp-then-rename (os.replace) for atomicity, (2) pathlib.Path used consistently, (3) all file handles in with blocks, (4) encoding always explicit (utf-8), (5) no hardcoded path separators.

Key tools for this area:
- pathlib (stdlib)
- atomicwrites

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Memory Management and Resource Leaks
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Memory Management and Resource Leaks

Area guidance:
Check: (1) all file/socket/lock resources in with blocks, (2) no __del__ for cleanup, (3) module-level globals don't accumulate unbounded data, (4) caches use lru_cache(maxsize=N).

Key tools for this area:
- tracemalloc (stdlib)
- objgraph

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Configuration / Settings / Secrets
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Configuration / Settings / Secrets

Area guidance (desktop-scoped):
Check: (1) no secrets hardcoded in source, (2) config loaded from .env or local config file (python-dotenv is sufficient), (3) missing required config values caught at startup with clear error. A full secret manager or pydantic-settings is optional for desktop tools.

Key tools for this area:
- python-dotenv
- configparser (stdlib)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:

  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Validation and Business Logic
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Validation and Business Logic

Area guidance:
Check: (1) all external inputs (file contents, user input) validated at boundaries, (2) domain rules expressed as explicit guard clauses with descriptive error messages, (3) validation errors carry context (which field failed and why), (4) pydantic or dataclass used for structured input instead of raw dicts.

Key tools for this area:
- pydantic v2
- hypothesis

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.

  ## Summary

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Security
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Security

Area guidance (desktop-scoped):
Check: (1) no eval() or exec() on user-controlled input, (2) subprocess calls use list form with shell=False, (3) file path inputs sanitized against directory traversal. SQL-injection and token-leak checks are only relevant if the tool uses a DB or external API.

Key tools for this area:
- bandit
- semgrep

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Threading / Background Jobs
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Threading / Background Jobs

Area guidance (desktop-scoped):
Check: (1) UI thread is never blocked by I/O or heavy computation, (2) shared mutable state protected by Lock or Queue, (3) worker threads have a stop mechanism and shut down cleanly. Full concurrency audit not required — focus on UI responsiveness.

Key tools for this area:
- threading (stdlib)
- concurrent.futures
- queue.Queue

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary

  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: UI Structure and Responsiveness
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
UI Structure and Responsiveness

Area guidance:
Check: (1) no blocking work on the main/UI thread, (2) business logic not embedded in widget event handlers, (3) UI state updates marshalled back to main thread via signals/slots or equivalent, (4) loading indicators shown during background work, (5) UI teardown waits for or cancels background workers.

Key tools for this area:
- PySide6 / PyQt6
- QThread / QRunnable

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Performance
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Performance

Area guidance:
Check: (1) hot paths avoid repeated attribute lookups and string concatenation in loops, (2) large data processed with generators rather than full lists, (3) caches have explicit size limits, (4) profile before optimizing — identify actual hotspots with cProfile or py-spy.

Key tools for this area:
- cProfile (stdlib)
- py-spy
- line_profiler

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Tests
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Tests

Area guidance (desktop-scoped):
Check: (1) unit tests cover core domain functions including boundary values and error paths, (2) tests are deterministic (no random, no datetime.now() without injection), (3) test names are readable and describe the scenario. Coverage target is pragmatic — focus on critical paths, not 80% across the board. Container-based integration tests not required.

Key tools for this area:
- pytest
- pytest-cov
- pytest-mock
- pyfakefs

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: write or extend tests for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Serialization and Deserialization
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for a desktop tool.

Current focus area:
Serialization and Deserialization

Area guidance:
Check: (1) pickle not used for untrusted input, (2) deserialized data schema-validated immediately on parse, (3) unknown fields explicitly rejected or ignored, (4) persistent formats include a version field.

Key tools for this area:
- pydantic v2
- jsonschema
- orjson

Execution rules:
